mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and higher recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicate strong consumer demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of co-occurring instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction with Rank IC below -0.03 when tested exclusively in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts combined with stagnant nonfarm payrolls indicates a synchronized slowdown in construction and labor demand, reducing future corporate revenue expectations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined housing and labor weakness signal fails to predict negative returns, with forward returns exceeding zero in greater than 50% of triggered cases during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity valuation discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 0.4% monthly, or the inflation-rate interaction shows no predictive power with Rank IC between -0.02 and 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production outpacing GDP growth signals improving supply-side efficiency and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth relative to GDPC1 shows no correlation with forward returns, or the signal predicts negative returns in greater than 55% of observations across all regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth coinciding with falling unemployment indicates robust labor market tightening and wage-driven consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual labor strength signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with forward returns negative in greater than 60% of triggered instances during mid-cycle phases.
